subject: Storage Layer — SQLite+vec (spoke-local) + Hub Postgres (fleet) + GitNexus integration bridge
title: Storage Layer — Spoke SQLite+vec, Hub Postgres, GitNexus Bridge
situation: Harness v4 produces structured harness data (bolts, patterns, checks, gate events, CG/PG/TG entries, test results, session metadata) with no queryable home. Currently this data lands in flat JSONL files (gate-log.jsonl, track.jsonl) that require full-file scans, can't do semantic search, and have no cross-fleet aggregation. GitNexus provides a code knowledge graph but harness events that reference file paths cannot link to it. The storage layer closes both gaps: gives harness data a proper queryable home and bridges harness events to code intelligence.
why: Without a storage layer: (1) pattern mining requires sequential file scans — O(n) per session, unusable at fleet scale; (2) CG semantic lookup is impossible without vector search; (3) cross-fleet halt detection requires hub-level aggregation that flat JSONL can't support; (4) GitNexus impact analysis cannot be triggered automatically when a pattern check references a failing file. With this spec: all four are solved with a clone-and-run spoke database, a hub aggregation tier, and a structured bridge to GitNexus.
audience: Historian — queries gate_log + patterns to detect recurring halts and approval rate trends
Expediter — queries pg_entries to produce ready-queue and timeline
Ozi — reads bolt status to surface certification health at session open
Pattern Gate Subagent — writes patterns + checks + bolt-verifications after each gate pass
Hub Gardener — aggregates spoke SQLite into Hub Postgres nightly for fleet-wide view
Navigator — queries CG + ToolGraph to route model selection
GitNexus MCP — receives symbol lookups triggered by harness event file_paths
use_cases: Historian mining: SELECT flow_id, step_id, COUNT(*) FROM gate_log WHERE disposition='halted' GROUP BY flow_id, step_id HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3 across 3+ sessions → draft teaching candidate
CG semantic lookup: embed situation text → vector query cg_embeddings → return top-k CG entries by cosine similarity → agent knows what solutions exist for this situation
Bolt health check: SELECT b.status, COUNT(c.result) FROM bolts b JOIN checks c ON c.bolt_id=b.id WHERE b.session_id=$current → any check.result=0 → bolt.status=needs_review
GitNexus bridge: pattern check fails on file tools/verify_engine.py → INSERT INTO gitnexus_refs → gitnexus_impact(symbol) → enriched impact stored with the check record
Hub fleet view: Hub Postgres query for halt patterns appearing in 3+ spokes → fleet-wide teaching candidate; result lug delivered to hub historian
Cross-session pattern matching: embed pattern evidence text → vector query pattern_embeddings → find similar patterns from prior sessions → surface to Historian as recurring pattern

spoke_database.install_prerequisite: sqlite-vec installed by Basher at session setup; session fails fast if extension missing

columns.certifier: TEXT NOT NULL — session.sub-agent identity that verified
columns.closed_at: TEXT — NULL until pattern completes; status only updated at close

bolts.note: Bolt is opened at session start. Status is updated (not emitted) when the pattern closes. One bolt per session×certifier; one bolt covers many patterns.

bolt_patterns.note: Join table — one bolt has many patterns; status reflects all checks across all patterns

columns.file_paths: JSON — files this pattern step touches; used for GitNexus bridge

columns.bolt_id: TEXT REFERENCES bolts(id) — NULL if not yet bolt-verified

checks.note: Atomic unit. result=0 is a loose bolt. Every unverified check degrades spoke health.

gate_log.note: Denormalized from patterns for fast cross-session halt analysis. Append-only. Also mirrors to WAI-Harness/spoke/patterns/gate-log.jsonl for non-DB readers.

columns.situation: TEXT NOT NULL — the triggering scenario (searchable via vector)
columns.solution: TEXT NOT NULL — how this is addressed
columns.file_paths: JSON — files that implement this capability
columns.symbol_refs: JSON — GitNexus symbol names that implement this capability

pg_entries.note: PathGraph (PG) — spoke narrative trajectory. Past=completed, future=open. Read by Expediter and Ozi.

columns.preferences: JSON — framing, tone, level of detail, preferred formats

tg_profiles.note: TasteGraph (TG) — communication optimization per target party.

columns.version: TEXT — owner version at time of run

columns.symbol: TEXT NOT NULL — GitNexus symbol name
columns.impact_radius: JSON — cached result from gitnexus_impact; refreshed on index update

gitnexus_refs.note: Bridge table. Populated when harness events reference file paths. Enables: check fails in file X → gitnexus_impact(X) → what else breaks.

search_baseline.engine: SQLite FTS5 — built in, zero dependency, offline
search_baseline.rule: FTS5 full-text search is the ALWAYS-ON baseline for cg_entries.situation, pattern evidence, and session summaries. Vector search is an enhancement layer on top. If no embedding provider is available, semantic queries degrade gracefully to FTS5 — they never fail. Observability never depends on a network call.

vector_tables.embedder_default: fastembed (ONNX runtime) — light, respects the project 'no heavy dependencies' constraint; no torch
vector_tables.embedder_routing: Embedding generation is ToolGraph-routed: if the spoke's access_matrix declares a hosted embedder (e.g. openai-embedding), use it; else use local fastembed; else disable vector tables and fall back to FTS5
vector_tables.cg_embeddings: vec_f32(384) — embedding of cg_entries.situation text; enables semantic CG lookup
vector_tables.session_embeddings: vec_f32(384) — embedding of session summary; enables 'find sessions like this one'
vector_tables.pattern_embeddings: vec_f32(384) — embedding of pattern evidence text; enables cross-session recurring pattern detection

hub_database.tables: Same schema as spoke SQLite with added wheel_id TEXT NOT NULL column on every table for spoke attribution
hub_database.hosting: Supabase-hosted Postgres + pgvector. Rationale: supabase-rest is already in the fleet ToolGraph access_matrix; pgvector is symmetric with the spoke's sqlite-vec; MCP-friendly; zero self-hosting ops burden. Self-hosting was rejected — adds an ops surface that fights 'remove friction'.
hub_database.sync: Spoke pushes new rows to Hub Postgres nightly via Basher sync job. Hub Postgres is a nightly aggregation target — NOT a real-time replica.
hub_database.mcp_exposure: Hub runs a Supabase/Postgres MCP server. Spokes query it for: fleet-wide gate_log halt patterns, mandated CG entries to sync into spoke SQLite, fleet bolt certification scores. Spoke degrades gracefully if hub is unreachable (uses local cache, marks sync-pending).

gitnexus_integration.principle: Complement, not duplicate. GitNexus owns code intelligence (symbols, relationships, call graphs, execution flows). SQLite+vec owns harness data. They compose via reference — harness events carry file_paths[], GitNexus resolves impact, enriched results stored back in SQLite+vec.
bridge_rules: When a pattern check disposition=halted and file_paths[] is non-empty: emit gitnexus_refs rows with ref_type=fails_in; trigger gitnexus_impact for each symbol in those files; cache in gitnexus_refs.impact_radius
When a CG entry references implementation symbols: populate cg_entries.symbol_refs[]; emit gitnexus_refs rows with ref_type=owns
When a test_result fails and the test references a file: emit gitnexus_refs rows with ref_type=fails_in
gitnexus_refs.impact_radius is a cache: refreshed when gitnexus index is updated (detect via gitnexus_detect_changes())
gitnexus_integration.prerequisite: GitNexus index must be current before bridge queries run. If stale (gitnexus_detect_changes() returns changes), re-index before populating impact_radius.

write_concurrency.principle: Event-driven advisors fire concurrently and all write harness.db; SQLite serializes writes (SQLITE_BUSY) and will contend/drop telemetry under load (external review CRITICAL B1).
rules: on init: PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL (concurrent readers + one writer) + PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000

SINGLE-WRITER QUEUE: all DB mutations go through one sequential writer worker (owned by Ozi/dispatcher); advisor processes stay non-blocking — they enqueue events, the worker drains them in order. No advisor writes harness.db directly.
the JSONL event journal is the durable floor (append-only, crash-safe); the SQLite write worker indexes from it, so a busy/locked DB never loses an event (it replays from the journal)
GitNexus mutations + DB updates that must be consistent are sequenced through the same worker with a compensating-rollback marker (no silent half-commit across the two stores)

retention_policy.principle: 'Every event emitted' + 'clone-and-run/offline' will bloat local SQLite unboundedly, degrading FTS5/vector + memory on Historian sweeps (external review HIGH B2). Bound it.

two_tier.raw_tier: atomic/trace events (checks, low-level gate/test events): auto-aggregated into daily metric summaries + pruned after 14 days locally (configurable)
two_tier.durable_tier: high-level entities (lugs, sessions, bolts, decisions, verdicts, certifications): preserved long-term
two_tier.hub_archive: raw events optionally shipped to Hub Postgres before local prune for fleet history; local stays lean

retention_policy.rule: pruning is itself a logged, observable action (a retention workflow), never silent deletion; aggregates preserve the trend signal Historian needs

workflow_state_telemetry_table: ADD table workflow_state_telemetry {workflow_id, step, status, actor, started_at, ended_at, span_ms, correlation_id, boundary_context} — explicit physical state-machine tracing so causality chains (observability spec) rest on real state rows, not just reconstructed event headers (external review A).
constraints: SQLite WAL mode + busy_timeout + a single-writer queue are mandatory — advisors never write harness.db directly (external review CRITICAL)
Raw/trace events auto-prune after a retention window (default 14d) with aggregation; durable entities preserved; pruning is logged not silent
SQLite+vec is spoke-local and clone-and-run — no network dependency; works offline
Hub Postgres requires hub connectivity; spokes must degrade gracefully if hub is unreachable
Schema migrations use versioned migration files under managed/migrations/; no destructive ALTER TABLE without a version bump
Vector extension (sqlite-vec) must be installed by Basher at session setup; session fails fast (not silently) if missing
Agents write to spoke SQLite only; they never write directly to Hub Postgres (Basher sync owns that path)
gate_log is append-only in both SQLite and JSONL mirror; no UPDATE or DELETE on gate_log rows
bolt.status is only updated (not set at creation) when the pattern that the bolt covers is completed
Checks with result=0 (loose bolts) must surface in spoke health report at session open — never silently ignored
stacking_inheritance: CG entries support stacking inheritance: hub-sourced mandated entries propagate to spoke SQLite on sync; spoke local entries can addend (new entries) or override (same id, source=local wins on configuration axis). Mandated tier always wins on behavior axis regardless of source.
tests: Fresh install: schema creation script creates all tables + vector indexes; all expected columns present; sqlite-vec SELECT works
Bolt lifecycle: open bolt → attach 3 checks (result=0) → verify 2 checks (result=1) → bolt.status=in_progress; verify 3rd → bolt.status=pass; reopen with 1 check.result=0 → bolt.status=needs_review
Loose bolt health report: session open with 2 unverified checks → wakeup brief shows 'N loose bolts' under certification health
CG semantic query: insert 5 CG entries with distinct situation text; embed each; vector query for a novel situation → top-2 results by cosine similarity returned correctly
Gate log: insert 5 halted events for same flow_id+step_id; Historian query returns count=5 → qualifies as halt pattern
GitNexus bridge: create check with file_paths=['tools/verify_engine.py'] and result=0 → gitnexus_refs row created with ref_type=fails_in; gitnexus_impact queried and impact_radius populated
Hub sync: spoke SQLite has 10 new gate_log rows since last sync; Basher sync pushes to Hub Postgres → 10 rows appear with correct wheel_id
Hub MCP query: spoke queries Hub Postgres via MCP for halt patterns in flow='closeout-v1' → returns aggregated count across fleet
Stacking: hub-sourced mandated CG entry synced to spoke → present in spoke cg_entries with source=hub; spoke adds local addendum with same situation → both present; query returns local addendum first
Graceful hub-unreachable: Hub Postgres MCP unavailable → spoke continues from local cache; session closeout marks sync_pending=true; no crash or data loss

health.test_coverage: none — spec only; impl lugs not yet created
resolved_decisions: Embedding model: 384-dim (bge-small/MiniLM class) via fastembed (ONNX, no torch) as local default; ToolGraph-routable to a hosted embedder; FTS5 always-on baseline so semantic search degrades gracefully and never depends on a network call. (S46)
Hub Postgres: Supabase-hosted + pgvector — aligns with existing fleet ToolGraph, symmetric with spoke sqlite-vec, MCP-friendly, zero ops burden. Self-hosting rejected. (S46)
open_questions: Migration file runner: inline Python in spoke tools/, or Basher-managed? Prefer Basher-managed to keep managed/ authoritative.
Pattern embeddings: embed the full evidence text or a summary? Full text risks noise; summary risks loss of signal. Start with full text, recalibrate after first Historian mining pass.

perceive: Read this spec fully. Check if WAI-Harness/spoke/managed/harness.db exists (probably not). Read spec-toolgraph-v1 for ToolGraph access_matrix (embedding model + Postgres access). Run gitnexus_detect_changes() to confirm GitNexus index is current. Read WAI-Spoke/advisors/historian/scan_state.json to understand current gate-log scan baseline.
execute: 1. Create tools/create_harness_db.py — idempotent schema creation script (all tables + vector indexes). 2. Create managed/migrations/001_initial.sql — the canonical schema migration. 3. Run create_harness_db.py on this spoke — harness.db created. 4. Backfill gate_log table from existing WAI-Spoke/patterns/gate-log.jsonl if present. 5. Backfill pg_entries from WAI-Spoke/lugs/ (lug objects) and WAI-Spoke/advisors/ozi/ (initiatives) if present. 6. Create tools/gitnexus_bridge.py — watches for new pattern rows with file_paths[], triggers gitnexus_impact, populates gitnexus_refs. 7. Create tools/hub_sync.py — reads new rows since last_sync_at, pushes to Hub Postgres via MCP. 8. Update Basher distribution manifest to include sqlite-vec install step. 9. Deliver impl lugs for each step to own incoming/.
verify: 1. python3 tools/create_harness_db.py runs without error; harness.db created; all tables present via .schema check. 2. Bolt lifecycle test passes (see tests). 3. CG semantic query returns correct top-2 results. 4. gitnexus_bridge.py: create synthetic check row with file_paths=['tools/verify_engine.py'] → gitnexus_refs populated. 5. hub_sync.py: stub Hub Postgres MCP response → 10 rows pushed correctly. 6. All 10 tests in tests[] pass.
